name: Las Palomas Trail
name_original: El Valle de Guadalentín — Circular por el Camino de Las Palomas
description: Hike the difficult 19.3-mile Las Palomas Trail in Parque Regional Calnegre y Cabo Cope, gaining 1368 feet over 8 hours.
region_intro: Hiking around Águilas offers diverse landscapes, from extensive coastlines with hidden coves and urban beaches to inland mountains. The region is characterized by protected natural areas like Cabo Cope and Calnegre Regional Park, featuring impressive cliffs and unique flora. Geological formations, including volcanic strata and sea-eroded caves, are prominent along the coastline, particularly in Las Cuatro Calas. Inland, the Sierra de la Almenara provides mountainous terrain and birdlife protection zones. Best hiking trails around Águilas The most popular hiking route is Tour of Calarreona beaches, a 4.3 miles (6.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 47 minutes to complete. This easy route explores the stunning beaches and crystal-clear waters of the Calarreona area. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cabo de Cope Trail (PR-MU 104), a moderate 4.7 miles (7.6 km) path. This route offers panoramic views from Cocón de Cope within the protected Cabo Cope and Calnegre Regional Park. Local hikers also love the Tour along the Cope Marina Trail (SL-MU 23), a 3.0 miles (4.8 km) trail leading through the wetland at Saladar Beach, often completed in about 1 hour 14 minutes. Hiking around Águilas is defined by diverse coastlines, protected natural parks, and inland mountain ranges. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy coastal strolls to more challenging mountain treks. The routes in Águilas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 370 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore Águilas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Águilas?
A: Águilas offers a wide variety of hiking opportunities, with over 90 trails recorded on komoot. These routes cater to all levels, from easy coastal walks to more challenging mountain treks.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Águilas?
A: The region boasts incredibly diverse landscapes. You'll find extensive coastlines with hidden coves and urban beaches, impressive cliffs, and unique geological formations like the volcanic strata and sea-eroded caves in the Protected Landscape of Las Cuatro Calas. Inland, areas like the Sierra de la Almenara offer mountainous terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Águilas?
A: Águilas benefits from a pleasant Mediterranean climate, making hiking enjoyable for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for exploring the trails. Even in winter, the mild climate can be ideal for outdoor activities.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or casual walkers in Águilas?
A: Yes, Águilas has numerous easy trails perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed stroll. Nearly half of the routes, around 48, are rated as easy. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e899539531/tour-along-the-cope-marina-trail-sl-mu-23" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour along the Cope Marina Trail (SL-MU 23)&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 4.8 km and leads through the wetland at Saladar Beach.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Águilas?
A: For experienced hikers looking for a challenge, Águilas offers several difficult routes, with 6 trails specifically rated as such. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e899552464/las-palomas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Palomas Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 31 km route with significant elevation gain, providing a true test of endurance within the Cabo Cope and Calnegre Regional Park.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Águilas?
A: Yes, there are several circular hiking routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31549434" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cope Tower – Cerro de la Cabrica Viewpoint loop from Playa del Hoyo&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate 9 km circuit. The Sendero del Baladre (PR-MU 112) in the Sierra de la Almenara is also a notable circular path.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Águilas?
A: Many of the easier coastal trails are suitable for families. Routes that explore the beaches and offer relatively flat terrain, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e898118419/tour-of-calarreona-beaches" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of Calarreona beaches&lt;/a&gt;, are excellent choices for a family outing.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in Águilas?
A: Hikers can discover a wealth of natural beauty and landmarks. Highlights include the impressive cliffs and coves of Cabo Cope, the unique sea-eroded caves at &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4085357" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Cocedores Beach&lt;/a&gt;, and the panoramic views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3141198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cocón de Cope&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features the protected landscapes of Las Cuatro Calas, including &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4201747" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Higuerica Beach&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites to explore on the trails?
A: Águilas is rich in history. You can hike to the historic Castle of San Juan de Águilas for panoramic views, or visit the 16th-century Cope Tower. Some routes also pass by the Hornillo Jetty, a reminder of the region's mining past, and you might even encounter remains of Roman settlements.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads in Águilas?
A: While Águilas has local transport, direct public transport access to all specific trailheads can be limited, especially for routes in more remote natural parks. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for routes that might serve areas closer to the starting points of some trails.

Q: Where can I find parking near the hiking trails in Águilas?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. For routes starting near urban areas or popular beaches, you'll often find designated parking lots. For trails within natural parks like Cabo Cope and Calnegre, there may be smaller parking areas or roadside spots. It's recommended to research specific trailheads for detailed parking information.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Águilas?
A: The hiking trails in Águilas are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.3 stars from over 370 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the stunning coastal views and the well-preserved natural environments.

Q: Can I expect to see wildlife while hiking in Águilas?
A: Yes, Águilas is a great place for wildlife spotting, particularly birds. The Sierra de la Almenara is part of the Natura 2000 Network and a ZEPA birdlife protection zone, home to species like the eagle owl and Bonelli's eagle. The Isla del Fraile, off the eastern coastline, is also recognized for its biodiversity.
tips: Nice route following the Camino de Las Palomas. You will go through the interior of the Guadalentín Valley region, at times with beautiful views of the coast and further away to the almond fields and the mountains of the area.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Las Palomas Trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is considered difficult. It's a long hike, covering over 31 kilometers with a significant elevation gain of around 417 meters, making it a demanding route that requires good physical condition and stamina.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Las Palomas Trail?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for the trailhead is not detailed, but as the trail is located within the Parque Regional Calnegre y Cabo Cope, you should look for designated parking areas near the start of the route within the park. It's advisable to check local park signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Is the Las Palomas Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Regulations for dogs within the Parque Regional Calnegre y Cabo Cope generally require dogs to be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. Always clean up after your pet and ensure they are prepared for a long, difficult hike.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Given its location in the Murcia region, the best time to hike the Las Palomas Trail is typically during the cooler months, from autumn to spring (October to May). Summers can be very hot, making the long, difficult hike more challenging and potentially unsafe due to heat exposure.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Parque Regional Calnegre y Cabo Cope?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no entrance fees to access the Parque Regional Calnegre y Cabo Cope itself. However, specific activities or certain areas within the park might require permits. It's always best to check the official park website or local information centers for the most current regulations before your visit.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: While specific terrain details for this exact route are not available, a difficult 31-kilometer hike with over 400 meters of elevation gain typically involves varied terrain. Expect a mix of ascents and descents, potentially rocky or uneven paths, and possibly some exposed sections. Sturdy hiking boots are highly recommended.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route primarily follows the Camino Las Palomas for almost its entire length. It also briefly intersects with sections of the Camino del Alto Guadalentín and the Transmurciana 09.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on the Las Palomas Trail?
faqs.answer: For a difficult, 31-kilometer hike, you should bring plenty of water (at least 2-3 liters), high-energy snacks, sturdy hiking boots, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), a first-aid kit, and appropriate clothing layers. A map or GPS device is also essential for navigation.

faqs.question: Are there any specific viewpoints or notable features along the way?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight along this route is the Camino Las Palomas itself, which forms the majority of the trail. As it traverses the Parque Regional Calnegre y Cabo Cope, you can expect scenic views of the coastal landscape and natural surroundings typical of the region.

faqs.question: Is the Las Palomas Trail a loop or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: Based on the tour data, the Las Palomas Trail is described as a circular route, meaning it is a loop trail. This allows you to experience varied scenery without retracing your steps.

faqs.question: What is the typical duration for completing this hike?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for completing this difficult hike is approximately 8 hours and 24 minutes. This is an average and can vary significantly based on your fitness level, pace, and the amount of time spent resting or enjoying the scenery.
